lessons.title: Why handle errors
lessons.body: When something goes wrong (dividing by zero, converting invalid text to a number), the program stops with an exception. `try`/`except` lets you catch that error and decide what to do, instead of the program simply crashing.

lessons.title: Catching specific error types
lessons.body: You can have several `except` blocks, one for each expected error type — this handles each problem differently, instead of one generic block that hides anything.

lessons.title: else, finally, and raising your own errors
lessons.body: The `else` block runs only if there was NO error; `finally` always runs, with or without an error (good for 'cleanup'). You can also raise your own errors with `raise` when a condition doesn't make sense for your program.

exercises.title: Safe division
exercises.prompt: Write a function `safe_divide(a, b)` that returns a / b, or 0 if b is zero (without crashing with ZeroDivisionError).
hints: Put the division inside a try.
Catch ZeroDivisionError and return 0 in that case.

exercises.title: Convert to a number
exercises.prompt: Write a function `to_number(text)` that returns int(text), or None if the text cannot be converted.
hints: int(text) raises ValueError when the text isn't a number.

exercises.title: Safely access an index
exercises.prompt: Write a function `item_or_none(items, index)` that returns items[index], or None if the index doesn't exist (IndexError).

exercises.title: Safely look up a key
exercises.prompt: Write a function `value_or_none(dictionary, key)` that returns dictionary[key], or None if the key doesn't exist (KeyError). Don't use the .get() method — use try/except.
hints: Accessing a key that doesn't exist raises KeyError.

exercises.title: Validate a grade
exercises.prompt: Write a function `validate_grade(grade)` that raises a ValueError with the message 'invalid grade' if grade is less than 0 or greater than 10, and returns the grade itself otherwise.

tests.name: grade 15 raises ValueError
grade -1 raises ValueError

hints: Use if grade < 0 or grade > 10: raise ValueError('invalid grade').
If it passed validation, just return grade.

exercises.title: Average without an empty list
exercises.prompt: Write a function `safe_average(numbers)` that returns the average of the list, or 0 if the list is empty (avoid ZeroDivisionError).
hints: sum(numbers)/len(numbers) breaks if the list is empty.
Handle it with try/except or check first with if.

exercises.title: Sum only valid numbers
exercises.prompt: Write a function `valid_sum(items)` that takes a list of texts, converts each one to a number and returns the sum of the ones successfully converted (ignore the ones that fail).
hints: Go through the list with for and use try/except inside the loop.
When it fails, just continue to the next item.

exercises.title: Non-negative age
exercises.prompt: Write a function `register_age(age)` that raises ValueError with the message 'invalid age' if age is negative, and returns 'registered' otherwise.

tests.name: age -1 raises ValueError

hints: Use raise ValueError('invalid age') inside an if.
